economic_notes: Nation operates retail cannabis dispensary without provincial licence; holds mineral rights, water resources and land; receives oil and gas royalties from designated reserve land; member of Yorkton Tribal Council Child and Family Services; has FNFA debenture secured by SIGA revenue for Recreation Centre project
notes: Fiscal year 2022 (ended March 31, 2022). Emphasis of matter paragraph in auditor report regarding non-compliance with Cannabis Act for unlicensed on-reserve retail cannabis dispensary — opinion not modified. Consolidated entities include Pheasant Rump Nakota First Nation #68, Pheasant Rump Nakota First Nation CMHC Housing, Pheasant Rump General Partner Ltd., and Pheasant Rump Master Development Limited Partnership. Project management cash of $3,204,392 held by capital project managers for ISC-funded capital projects included in cash resources. Large increase in deferred revenue ($4.03M vs $1.44M prior year) driven by ISC capital project funding. FNFA debenture and promissory note ($2.32M) newly drawn during year for Recreation Centre and infrastructure. Gain on disposal of TCA $3,357 included in other income. No schedule of remuneration included in extracted text.
